satire_attempt: Three monitors. The cursor blinks. The senior engineer has not yet spoken. In this profound silence, the engineer learns nothing — because, as our sacred text reminds us, sometimes a verse is just a stage direction. Truly the deepest insight of all: that some moments are setup. Subscribe for next week's verse, where Krishna also says nothing, and we charge you to learn from his silence.
verdict_reason: My satire only works by attacking the verse for being honestly modest — and the verse pre-empts the mockery by openly stating it is not a teaching. Mocking 'the verse admits it is just scaffolding' is unfair to the verse; it is exactly the kind of low-key, anti-self-help move that defeats trivialization. PASS.
